description: Use for the Real-World Reference contents
---

## Strv 103
The Strv 103, also known as the S-Tank, was a Swedish main battle tank designed around a very low silhouette and a fixed gun mounting.

![1](public\images\sw_strv_103c.jpg)
![2](public\images\STRV-103_demonstrating_at_P7_Revingehed,_24th_April_2022.jpg)
![3](public\images\sw_strv_103c.jpg)

### Overview
Unlike traditional tanks with a rotating turret, the Strv 103 aimed the gun by traversing the entire vehicle and adjusting suspension. That unusual layout made the tank compact, hard to spot, and highly distinctive.

### Historical reference
The tank entered Swedish army service in the 1960s during the Cold War. It reflected a defensive doctrine focused on ambush tactics, survivability, and firing from prepared positions rather than open warfare.


## Centurion Action X
The "Centurion Action X" is a British experimental tank design. It originated in 1955 as an upgrade project to improve the standard Centurion’s ballistic protection by replacing the older turret with a heavily sloped, "mantletless" design.

![1](public\images\sw_strv_103c.jpg)
![2](public\images\STRV-103_demonstrating_at_P7_Revingehed,_24th_April_2022.jpg)
![3](public\images\sw_strv_103c.jpg)

### Overview
Because the original vehicle never passed the prototype and ballistic testing stages, it is famous today primarily as a virtual combat vehicle in games like World of Tanks.

### Historical reference
Initiated around 1955, the project sought to solve the prominent weak points of the classic Centurion turret—specifically the large, vulnerable gun mantlet. By removing the traditional mantlet and reshaping the turret, British engineers were able to create a much more heavily angled front profile that vastly increased shell deflection.


## Tiger 1
The Panzerkampfwagen VI, commonly known as the Tiger I, was a formidable German heavy tank used in World War II.

![1](public\images\sw_strv_103c.jpg)
![2](public\images\STRV-103_demonstrating_at_P7_Revingehed,_24th_April_2022.jpg)
![3](public\images\sw_strv_103c.jpg)

### Overview
Tiger I is arguably the most famous tank of the Second World War, the impenetrable armour, powerful gun and huge size of the Tiger made it a legend in its time. The tank did have its problems. It was unreliable and caught fire easily.


### Historical reference
Introduced in mid-1942, the Tiger I was deployed primarily on the Eastern and Western Fronts, as well as in North Africa. Because of its heavy armor and long-range firepower, it was incredibly effective in defensive operations and was highly successful in taking out Allied and Soviet armor.
